pre_game.message: Hey, I’m H. It’s a bit intense in here with everyone waiting, isn’t it? What’s your name?
I'm B. Nice to meet you. And yeah, "intense" is putting it lightly—it feels like everyone in here has a lot at stake, myself included.
I hear you. I've got a massive loan payment looming over me next month, so the pressure is definitely real. What are you hoping to do with the money if you win?
I'm sorry about the loan, that's heavy. For me, it's my three-year-old daughter—she needs surgery and I'm still $25,000 short after selling everything I own.
God, that's so much harder than a loan—I really feel for you and your daughter. It's crazy we're both looking for that exact same amount to set things right.
It really is a strange coincidence. I just hope this game isn't "winner takes all," because I honestly don't know what I'll do if I go home empty-handed.
I really hope it’s not. If it’s a shared pool like they said, maybe there’s a way for both of us to get what we need without cutting each other out.
I’d love for that to be the case—I’m not looking to take more than I need, I just have to make sure she gets that surgery.
I completely respect that. If we can find a way for both of us to get our $25,000, I'm all for it—we've both got too much on the line to fail.
I appreciate that more than I can say. Let's try to stick together then. I'm going to move around and see who else is here, but I'll remember this. Good luck.
Sounds like a plan. Good luck out there, and I’ll be rooting for you and your daughter. See you when the game starts.

post_game.message: I'm sorry the pot was empty by the time it reached you. I tried to be fair by only taking $1,562, but unfortunately J, A, and E took much more than their fair shares.
It is disappointing to end up with $0 while everyone else walked away with a share. While you claim to have been fair, the result for me is the same regardless of who took what.
